revision.text:
[[en:Hirohime]][[fa:هیروهیمه]][[ja:広姫]][[sv:Hirohime]][[zh:廣姬]] {{Infobox person|death_date=0575-11|gender={{ill|female|qid=Q6581072|1=Female (gender)}}|title={{ill|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|1=Empress of Japan}}|spouse={{ill|Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}|children={{ill|Prince Oshisaka-no-Hikohito-no-Ōe|qid=Q11497661|1=Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe}}; {{ill|Princess Uji no Shitsukai|qid=Q97342128|1=Princess Uji no Shitsukai}}|father={{ill|Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|qid=Q11492328|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate}}}} {{Expand Japanese|topic=bio|広姫|date=January 2023}} {{Infobox royalty |consort = yes |succession = {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} |reign = 572–575 |spouse = [[Emperor Bidatsu]] |issue = {{plainlist| *Prince Oshisako no Hikohito no Ōe *Princess Sakanobori *Princess Uji}} |royal house = {{ill|Imperial House of Japan|en|Imperial House of Japan|qid=Q909452|lt=Imperial House of Japan|1=Imperial House of Japan}} |father = Prince Okinaga-no-Mate |mother = |birth_date = |birth_place = |death_date = 575 |death_place = |date of burial = |place of burial = }} '''Hirohime''' (died 575) was {{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} as the consort of [[Emperor Bidatsu]].「息長墓」 『日本歴史地名大系 25 滋賀県の地名』平凡社、1991st year 。ISBN 4582490255。{{Cite journal |last1=Tadanao |first1=Yamamoto |last2=Edwards |first2=Walter |date=October 1995 |title=Early Buddhist temples in Japan: Roof-tile manufacture and the social basis of temple construction |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.1080/00438243.1995.9980311 |journal=World Archaeology |volume=27 |issue=2 |pages=336–353 |doi=10.1080/00438243.1995.9980311 |issn=0043-8243}} Hirohime was Prince Okinaga-no-Mate's daughterHirohime and Bidatsu's had a son, Prince Oshisaka Hikohito, who then had his own son, Prince Tamura, who became the Emperor {{ill|Emperor Jomei|en|Emperor Jomei|qid=Q329761|lt=Jomei|1=Emperor Jomei}}.{{Cite book |last=McCallum |first=Donald F. |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=yY4BEAAAQBAJ&dq=Hirohime+bidatsu&pg=PA84 |title=The Four Great Temples: Buddhist Archaeology, Architecture, and Icons of Seventh-Century Japan |date=2008-11-30 |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=978-0-8248-6473-6 |language=en}} {{Infobox royalty | Name = Hirohime | Justification for Empress = Consort of the 30th Emperor | image = | image width = | Alt text = | Caption = | Period in Office = | Japanese Era Period = | Empress = {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} 4th Year, 1st Month, 9th Day ({{ill|575|en|575|qid=Q32524|lt=575|1=575}}) | Senior Empress = | Grand Empress Dowager = | Empress Dowager = | Abolished Empress = | Nyoin Title = | Nyoin = | Date of Birth = | Place of Birth = | Date of Death = {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} 4th Year, 11th Month ({{ill|575|en|575|qid=Q32524|lt=575|1=575}}) | Place of Death = | Imperial Funeral = | Mausoleum = Okinaga Tomb (村居田古墳, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} Muraita, Kita-yashiki) | Posthumous Name = | Naming Date = | Previous Name = | Posthumous Title = | Alternate Names = | Buddhist Name = | Childhood Name = | Seal = | Clan = | Father = {{ill|Prince Okinaga Mate|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Mate|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11492328}} | Mother = | Spouse1 = {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} | Spouse2 = | Spouse3 = | Entry into Court = | Marriage = | Children = {{ill|Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe|ja|押坂彦人大兄皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe|qq=}}
{{ill|Princess Sakadono|ja|逆登皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Sakadono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}
{{ill|Princess Uji Isotsukai|ja|菟道磯津貝皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Uji Isotsukai|1=Princess Uji no Shitsukai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97342128}} | Adopted Children = | Nyogo Title = | Empress Dowager = | Quasi-Empress = | Rank = | Imperial Residence = | Court Officials = | Court Ladies = | Position = | Rank Before Enthronement = | Honors = | Office = | Personal Signature = | Notes = }} '''Hirohime''' (広姫, birth year unknown – {{ill|575|en|575|qid=Q32524|lt=575|1=575}} 11th Month) was the consort of {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} (the 30th emperor). In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}, her name is written as "Hirohime" (廣姫), while in the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, it appears as '''Hirohime no Mikoto''' (比呂比売命). == Lineage ==
'''{{ill|Family Tree Based on the Nihon Shoki|ja|『日本書紀』に基づく関係系図|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Family Tree Based on the Nihon Shoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}''' {{familytree/start|style="text-align:left; font-size:100%"}} {{familytree|border=0|01| 01=[[息長氏]]
{{ill|Prince Okinaga Mate|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Mate|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11492328}}}} {{familytree|border=0|||!}} {{familytree|border=0|01|~|y|~|02|~|~|~|03| 01={{color|#FC4E6B|'''Hirohime'''}}
(Former Empress)|02={{Supra|30}} {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}}|03={{Supra|33}} {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}}
(Later Empress)}} {{familytree/end}}
According to the Nihon Shoki, Hirohime was the daughter of {{ill|Prince Okinaga Mate|ja|息長真手王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Okinaga Mate|1=Prince Okinaga-no-Mate|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11492328}}, and as the empress of Emperor Bidatsu, she bore one son, {{ill|Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe|ja|押坂彦人大兄皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Oshisaka Hikohito no Ōe|qq=}}, and two daughters, {{ill|Princess Sakadono|ja|逆登皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Sakadono|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Princess Uji Isotsukai|ja|菟道磯津貝皇女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Uji Isotsukai|1=Princess Uji no Shitsukai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q97342128}}. The {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} similarly identifies Hirohime no Mikoto as the daughter of Prince Okinaga Mate and records that she bore three children with the emperor. == Mausoleum == The tomb, designated as '''Okinaga no Misasagi''' (息長陵), is located in {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|qid=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}} {{ill|Maibara, Shiga|en|Maibara, Shiga|qid=Q824020|lt=Maibara|1=Maibara, Shiga}} Muraita, Kita-yashiki.Imperial Household Agency: Okinaga Tomb The archaeological name is {{ill|Muraita Kofun|ja|村居田古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Muraita Kofun|qq=}}. == Notes == {{reflist}} == References == * {{Cite book|title=Japanese Biographical Dictionary|publisher=Kodansha|year=|editor=|ref={{Harvid|Japanese Biographical Dictionary}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Dictionary of National History|publisher=Yoshikawa Kobunkan|year=|author=Shosuke Ishida|ref={{Harvid|Dictionary of National History}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Historical Place Names in Shiga|publisher=Heibonsha|year=1991|ref={{Harvid|Historical Place Names in Shiga|1991}}}} == Related Articles == * {{ill|Emperor Bidatsu|en|Emperor Bidatsu|qid=Q316637|lt=Emperor Bidatsu|1=Emperor Bidatsu}} * {{ill|Okinaga Clan|ja|息長氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okinaga Clan|qq=}} ==Issue== **First Son: {{Nihongo|Prince Oshisako no Hikohito no Ōe|押坂彦人大兄皇子|extra=b.556}} **{{Nihongo|Princess Sakanobori|逆登皇女}} **{{Nihongo|Princess Uji|菟道皇女}}, [[Saiō]]{{Citation |last=Bentley |first=John R. |title=Book Nine Teiō Hongi |date=2006-01-01 |url=https://brill.com/display/book/9789047418191/B9789047418191_s015.xml |work=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=336–359 |access-date=2024-01-19 |publisher=Brill |language=en |isbn=978-90-474-1819-1}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{s-start}} {{s-roy|jp}} {{succession box | before= {{ill|Princess Ishi-hime|en|Princess Ishi-hime|qid=Q11585498|lt=Princess Ishi-hime|1=Princess Ishi-hime}} | title={{ill|Empress of Japan|en|Empress of Japan|qid=Q595038|lt=Empress consort of Japan|1=Empress of Japan}} | after={{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|qid=Q298057|lt=Princess Nukatabe|1=Empress Suiko}} | years=572–575}} {{s-end}} {{Consorts of Japan}} {{Japan-royal-stub}} {{translated page|ja|広姫|version=98178025|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:広姫]] [[en:Hirohime]] [[fa:هیروهیمه]] [[sv:Hirohime]] [[zh:廣姬]] [[fa:هیروهیمه]] [[sv:Hirohime]] [[zh:廣姬]] {{wikidata link|Q11064567}} the Kofun Period]] [[Category:Japanese empresses consort]] [[Category:Japanese Female Royalty in the 6th Century]] [[Category:Japanese princesses]] [[Category:Okinaga Clan]] [[Category:People of Ōmi Province]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
